name: Philip J. Corso
summary: Retired U.S. Army lieutenant colonel and coauthor of The Day After Roswell, which alleged military transfer and reverse engineering of recovered technology.
profileStatus: Former Army officer and influential historical claimant
profileSummary: Retired U.S. Army lieutenant colonel and coauthor of The Day After Roswell, which alleged military transfer and reverse engineering of recovered technology.
background: U.S. Army officer from 1942 to 1963, former National Security Council staff member, and former chief of the Army's Foreign Technology desk.
inclusionNote: Included because his documented Army career and bestselling book made him one of the most influential historical figures in Roswell and reverse-engineering literature. The book's extraordinary claims remain disputed.

primarySourceLinks.label: The Day After Roswell publisher page
